sections.title: What are dependent prepositions?
sections.content:
**Dependent prepositions** are fixed prepositions that belong to a particular verb, noun or adjective.

You cannot change them without sounding wrong.

Example: interested **in** (not *interested on* or *interested at*).

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
• I'm interested **in** history.
• It depends **on** the weather.
• She's good **at** maths.
• We complained **about** the noise.

Changing the preposition usually makes the sentence wrong.

sections.title: Why they matter
sections.content:
Dependent prepositions are very common in exams (especially gap-fill and open cloze).

There is no single rule — you have to learn each pair as a chunk (e.g. depend **on**, interested **in**).

sections.title: Verbs + preposition
sections.content:
Common examples:
• depend **on**
• listen **to**
• wait **for**
• apply **for**
• complain **about**
• suffer **from**

sections.title: Nouns + preposition
sections.content:
Common examples:
• reason **for**
• solution **to**
• answer **to**
• increase **in**
• interest **in**
• relationship **with**

sections.title: Adjectives + preposition
sections.content:
Common examples:
• interested **in**
• good **at**
• afraid **of**
• proud **of**
• tired **of**
• responsible **for**
• worried **about**

sections.title: Useful pattern
sections.content:
Often the noun follows the same preposition as the verb/adjective:

interested **in** → interest **in**
complain **about** → complaint **about**

sections.title: How to learn them
sections.content:
There is no single rule — you have to learn each combination as a **chunk**. Try these strategies:

• Write the preposition together with the word: *depend **on***, not just *depend*.
• When you meet a new one, add it to a list under Verb / Noun / Adjective.
• Full reference lists are in the **Dependent Prepositions** section under Reference.

questions.question: Which preposition completes the phrase? 'It depends ____ the weather.'
Choose the correct noun + preposition pair.
Which preposition completes the phrase? 'She's afraid ____ spiders.'
Which option completes the sentence? 'They complained ____ the noise.'
Which preposition completes the phrase? 'It depends ____ the weather.'
Choose the correct noun + preposition pair.
Which preposition completes the phrase? 'She's afraid ____ spiders.'
Which option completes the sentence? 'They complained ____ the noise.'
She's good ____ maths. Which preposition?
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective): good **at** a subject or skill.

questions.question: We're looking ____ a new flat. Which preposition?
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb): look **for** something (search for it).

questions.question: She applied ____ the job. Which preposition?
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb): apply **for** a job or course.

questions.question: We're waiting ____ the bus. Which preposition?
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb): wait **for** someone or something.

questions.question: He's proud ____ his team. Which preposition?
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective): proud **of** someone or something.

questions.question: She's good ____ maths.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective): good **at** a subject or skill.

questions.question: We're looking ____ a new flat.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb): look **for** something (search for it).

questions.question: She applied ____ the job.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb): apply **for** a job or course.

questions.question: We're waiting ____ the bus.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb): wait **for** someone or something.

questions.question: He's proud ____ his team.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective): proud **of** someone or something.

questions.question: I'm tired ____ working late every night.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (adjective): tired **of** something (you've had enough).

gapfill_common.title: Further practice: Verbs, nouns, and adjectives + preposition

questions.question: We need a solution ____ this problem.
She has a good relationship ____ her boss.
He has a lot of experience ____ teaching.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (noun): experience **in** a field (experience in teaching).

questions.question: We congratulated her ____ winning the prize.
He suffers ____ severe headaches.
questions.explanation: Dependent preposition (verb): suffer **from** an illness or problem.
